assets.name: Pre-Creation Design Report
assets.notes: Animated HTML report covering game concept, core loop, entities, milestone plan, risks, and future ideas — written before implementation began.

reports.summary: Built the complete Stormtown game loop from scratch: procedural town map, player movement, tornadoes, zombies, Sam NPC rescue, helicopter escape, and all screens.
changes: Phaser 3 + Vite project setup with portrait orientation lock
Procedural town grid generator (40×60 tiles, 1280×1920 world) with road network and building blocks
Player entity: 160 px/s base speed, 240 px/s sprint with stamina drain/regen, 3 HP system, 1.2s invulnerability frames

Tornado entity: semi-guided AI targeting player with randomized waypoints, 200px suction zone (slow + pull force), 44px kill zone (instant death), spinning animation with debris particles, difficulty escalation (1 → 4 tornadoes at 60/120/180s)
Zombie entity: wander AI with seeded timer, chase AI at 180px detection range, physics collider with buildings and each other
Sam NPC at NE quadrant with beacon pulse animation and rescue dialogue popup
Helicopter pad at north center with pulsing ring animation and overlap win zone
Storm surge mechanic: all tornadoes speed up ×1.2 when key is picked up
InputManager: unified WASD/arrow keys + touch virtual joystick (left half) + sprint button (right half)
UIScene (parallel scene): HP hearts, elapsed timer, stamina bar, objective arrow compass, key badge, storm surge warning
Title screen with animated cloud strips, lightning flash effect, best time display
Win screen with escape time, new best time detection, localStorage persistence
Game Over screen with cause of death (tornado vs zombie distinction)
Camera follow with world bounds, 12% lerp for smooth tracking
Procedurally generated textures for all entities (no external image files required)
Sky parallax overlay with scrolling dark cloud layer at varying scroll factors
bugs: Tornadoes can clip through building corners (no building collision on tornado movement — intentional for now, but makes the tornado feel slightly unphysical)
Virtual joystick start position locks to first touch point — dragging fast can sometimes cause the knob to lag behind slightly on low-end devices
Sam beacon position uses tile coords that may occasionally land inside a building edge if building generation shifts — needs boundary check
Zombie respawn not yet implemented — once 12 zombies wander off-screen they don't come back
The Phaser UIScene receives per-frame data via polling (onUpdate call from GameScene update) — this works but is tightly coupled; a cleaner event system would be better
Sprint button zone is right half of screen which can conflict with tapping UI elements on some layouts
suggestions: Add footstep or running sound that changes pitch with sprint for player feedback
Tornado roar should get louder as it approaches — proximity audio would greatly amplify tension
Add a brief tutorial overlay on first play explaining controls and objective
Sprint button should be a visible on-screen button with tactile feel, not just a right-half tap zone
Buildings should have varying heights visually (darker/lighter gradient) to suggest 3D depth
Add a panic meter or heartbeat effect when tornado is within 300px
recommendations: Add zombie respawn system from map edges before Milestone 2 — the game gets too easy after initial zombies wander away
Decouple UIScene from GameScene with an event emitter (Phaser.Events.EventEmitter) rather than direct function calls — current coupling makes it brittle
Consider adding a StaticTilemapLayer for buildings to get tilemap rendering performance rather than raw Graphics objects, especially on mobile
InputManager should cache scale factor instead of reading it per event — minor perf win on mobile
Test tornado suction force stacking with 4 tornadoes — may need a cap to prevent physics explosion
ideas: Weather progression: rain starts light and intensifies, flooding some roads (slows movement)
Radio broadcasts: intermittent text overlays of emergency broadcasts add atmosphere
Car mechanic: find a working car to drive faster but harder to maneuver in tight streets
Multiple survivors: more friends to rescue, each adds a bonus (stamina boost, speed boost)
Daily challenge mode: fixed seed with leaderboard via URL hash sharing
Tornado path history: brief trail showing where tornado came from to help predict movement
reports.notes: All visuals are procedurally generated in BootScene via Phaser Graphics — no external image assets needed. The town layout is deterministic with seed 42, so the same map appears every run. Changing the seed would give variety. The game runs entirely in-browser with no server requirements.
